top_stories.title: The Back Door Attackers Know About — and Most Security Teams Still Haven’t Closed
top_stories.summary: The article examines persistent OAuth tokens granted to third-party AI tools and productivity apps connected to Google and Microsoft as an undermonitored attack vector. These tokens have no expiration date, bypass MFA and perimeter controls, and are largely unmanaged in most organizations.

top_stories.title: Omada Launches Omada Identity Cloud Private for Regulated Enterprises and Government Organizations
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has launched "Omada Identity Cloud Private," a new deployment option that runs the full Omada Identity Cloud platform within the customer's own tenant. The offering is designed for regulated enterprises and government organizations requiring specific data sovereignty and auditability boundaries.

top_stories.title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
top_stories.summary: Attackers are deploying the CloudZ RAT and a new plugin called Pheno to abuse Windows Phone Link, enabling them to intercept SMS messages and bypass two-factor authentication. The attack exploits the Windows-based bridge between PCs and smartphones, making it difficult to detect while effectively circumventing SMS-based 2FA controls.

top_stories.title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
top_stories.summary: A critical authentication-bypass vulnerability in cPanel has been disclosed, with multiple proof-of-concept exploits emerging shortly after. A researcher claims zero-day exploitation of the flaw has been active for at least a month, potentially affecting millions of systems.

top_stories.title: CISA gives feds four days to patch Ivanti flaw exploited as zero-day
top_stories.summary: CISA issued an emergency directive requiring U.S. federal agencies to patch a high-severity zero-day vulnerability in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) within four days. The flaw is actively being exploited in the wild, triggering the accelerated remediation timeline under CISA's known exploited vulnerabilities catalog.

news.title: Helping North Korean IT remote workers is becoming a fast track to prison
news.summary: Two U.S. nationals were sentenced to 18 months in prison for running "laptop farms" that helped North Korean IT workers fraudulently gain employment at nearly 70 American companies, generating over $1.2 million for Pyongyang. The cases highlight identity fraud and insider threat risks, as the schemes involved falsifying identities to bypass employment verification and access controls at U.S. organizations.

news.title: New Linux PamDOORa Backdoor Uses PAM Modules to Steal SSH Credentials
news.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new Linux backdoor called PamDOORa that abuses Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM) to steal SSH credentials and maintain persistent access. The malware is sold on a Russian cybercrime forum for $1,600 and uses a magic password and TCP port combination to enable covert, persistent SSH access on compromised systems.

news.title: A new frontier: Identity stack evolves for agentic systems
news.summary: Computer Weekly's Security Think Tank examines how the identity stack is evolving to support agentic AI systems. The article explores the shift of identity infrastructure toward functioning as a real-time control plane to manage authentication and authorization for autonomous AI agents.

news.title: PCPJack Credential Stealer Exploits 5 CVEs to Spread Worm-Like Across Cloud Systems
news.summary: Researchers disclosed PCPJack, a credential theft framework that exploits five CVEs to spread worm-like across cloud systems while harvesting credentials from cloud, container, developer, productivity, and financial services. The toolset exfiltrates stolen credentials through attacker-controlled infrastructure, targeting exposed cloud environments and displacing competing threat actor artifacts linked to TeamPCP.

news.title: World Passkey Day: Advancing passwordless authentication
news.summary: Microsoft published a blog post for World Passkey Day highlighting its efforts to advance passkey adoption as a replacement for passwords. The post covers Microsoft's passwordless authentication initiatives, including reducing phishing risk and improving sign-in security through passkey technology.

news.title: 5 leading enterprise password managers to consider
news.summary: The article reviews five enterprise password managers, outlining features and capabilities admins should consider. It covers criteria for evaluating password management tools in an enterprise context.

news.title: Semperis Names John Podboy Chief Information Security Officer
news.summary: Semperis has appointed John Podboy as its Chief Information Security Officer. Podboy will focus on risk, resilience, compliance, and agentic AI-driven cyber defense at the identity-focused cybersecurity company.

news.title: Your AI Agents Are Already Inside the Perimeter. Do You Know What They're Doing?
news.summary: Gartner has released its inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents, confirming that enterprise AI agent adoption is outpacing governance and policy controls. The report addresses the identity security challenge of governing AI agents that operate inside enterprise perimeters with access to systems and data.

news.title: Windows Phone Link Exploited by CloudZ RAT to Steal Credentials and OTPs
news.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have detailed an attack using the CloudZ RAT and a previously undocumented plugin called Pheno to steal credentials and one-time passwords (OTPs). The attack exploited Windows Phone Link to facilitate credential theft, targeting authentication factors including OTPs.

news.title: The AWS MCP Server is now generally available
news.summary: AWS has announced the general availability of the AWS MCP Server, a managed remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) server designed to give AI agents and coding assistants secure, authenticated access to AWS services. The server is part of AWS's Agent Toolkit and provides authentication and access control mechanisms for AI agent interactions with AWS infrastructure.

news.title: Keeper Security Research Reveals 89% of IT Leaders Struggle to Manage Growing Identity Footprint Amid AI Expansion
news.summary: Keeper Security released a global study of 3,200 cybersecurity decision-makers finding that 89% of IT leaders struggle to manage growing identity footprints amid AI expansion. The research found 96% of respondents report that disconnected identity tools are creating exploitable security gaps, with AI adoption accelerating identity sprawl.

news.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
news.summary: A proof-of-concept exploit demonstrates that Microsoft Edge stores passwords in process memory, allowing an attacker with admin privileges to extract stored credentials. The vulnerability poses an enterprise risk by enabling credential theft that could be leveraged for further malicious activity.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted to conducting SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and enabled the theft of tens of millions in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying instead on router flaws to silently siphon authentication credentials at scale.
